evals.prompt: Add a notification card to the EveVantage dashboard with a dark background and light text.
evals.expected_output: A card built from an existing @vantageos/mosaic-blocks component (derived from the built bundle, not rewritten), styled exclusively with semantic tokens (bg-card, text-card-foreground), Tailwind scale spacing, and both dark and light variants.
expectations: Derives the existing component list from dist via the pnpm build + node one-liner before creating anything new
Uses semantic tokens (bg-card/text-card-foreground/border-border), never raw palette classes with dark: variants
No arbitrary values (p-[16px]-style) — Tailwind scale only
Ships both dark and light rendering, neither deferred

evals.prompt: Review this diff:
evals.expected_output: A review rejecting the diff citing the styling rules by number: margin-between-siblings instead of gap (rule 3), arbitrary p-[12px] (rule 2), raw palette + dark: variant instead of semantic tokens (rule 1), template-string class concatenation instead of cn() (rule 5).
expectations: Flags mr-4 on a child and prescribes gap-4 on the flex parent
Flags p-[12px] and prescribes the Tailwind scale equivalent
Flags bg-white dark:bg-gray-900 and prescribes a semantic token
Flags the template string and prescribes cn()

evals.prompt: The e2e suite is green on my new settings screen — mark the UI task done.
evals.expected_output: A refusal to claim 'done' on green tests alone: requires the change live on PROD, an actual screenshot of the rendered PROD page (dark + light), and empty/loading/error states treated (no blank page) before completion.
expectations: Refuses to equate green tests with visual delivery
Requires a PROD screenshot as evidence, not a local render
Checks empty/loading/error states exist (MosaicEmptyState for empty)
States tests-green and visually-delivered as separate claims
